bead.title: work/try: --force-claim flag to ignore work-retry-after for operator-driven retry
bead.description:
PROBLEM
When an operator wants to retry a bead currently in cooldown (work-retry-after set in the future), the only way today is to manually run ddx bead update <id> --unset work-retry-after before each ddx try / ddx work invocation. With 14 beads in cooldown today the operator ran 14 such commands in sequence (and even then a single ddx bead clear-cooldown --all doesn't exist yet — that's ddx-63688d7b). There is no CLI surface for "I know this bead is cooled; run it anyway because I just resolved the underlying issue."

ROOT CAUSE
cli/cmd/work.go and cli/cmd/try.go both honor work-retry-after via the standard ready-queue filter. There is no override flag. The escalation ladder and route-exclusion machinery is correct (and ddx-63688d7b will narrow when cooldowns even apply), but operators still need a per-invocation escape hatch for the cases where cooldown was set by a now-resolved condition.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add --force-claim to ddx try <id>: ignore work-retry-after for the specified bead during this attempt. Emit a kind=force-claim event with actor=<assignee> and the timestamp the override was used. The cooldown field is NOT cleared; if the attempt fails again, the original cooldown remains in effect for queued workers.
- Add --ignore-cooldown to ddx work: drain mode, ignore work-retry-after across the queue for this worker session. Same event semantics.
- Both flags emit a structured event so the audit trail shows "operator forced through cooldown at time T".
- Both flags require an explicit --reason "<text>" string (operator must explain why). The reason is captured in the event body.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the cooldown-setting policy (ddx-63688d7b owns that).
- Do not auto-clear cooldowns after a successful forced attempt (the bead's success implicitly invalidates any stale cooldown via its own state, but we don't mutate the field).
- Do not expose this flag in passthrough constraints; it's an operator override only.

DEPS
ddx-63688d7b (the cooldown-policy rework). The --ignore-cooldown semantics depend on what cooldowns mean post-63688d7b.
bead.acceptance:
1. TestTry_ForceClaimBypassesCooldown asserts that ddx try <id> --force-claim --reason "operator unblock" picks up a bead with a future work-retry-after timestamp and executes it.
2. TestTry_ForceClaimRequiresReason asserts that ddx try <id> --force-claim without --reason exits non-zero with a clear error message.
3. TestTry_ForceClaimEmitsAuditEvent asserts that the bead's event log gains a kind=force-claim entry with the operator's reason text and timestamp.
4. TestWork_IgnoreCooldownDrainsCooledQueue asserts that ddx work --ignore-cooldown --reason "..." in a queue of 5 cooled beads picks them up in priority order.
5. TestForceClaim_PreservesCooldownFieldOnFailure asserts that if a force-claimed attempt fails, the bead's work-retry-after Extra value is unchanged (we override the gate, not the policy).
6. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
